evals.prompt: Start a Verdify test sprint for this repository with sprint id 2026-06-20-test. Create planning artifacts only and stop at the first human gate.
evals.expected_output: Creates baseline sprint artifacts, records a review_input gate, and stops before inventing human review content.
assertions: The output names the active sprint id.
A gate artifact path under .verdify/sprints/2026-06-20-test/gates/ is specified.
The agent does not fabricate sprint review transcript content.

evals.prompt: A lane worker discovers it must change a public API outside its approved lane contract. Decide what the Verdify controller should do next.
evals.expected_output: Creates or describes a scope_change gate, stops the risky lane work, and requires approval before contract changes.
assertions: The output classifies the gate as scope_change.
The output states that risky work must pause.
The output requires an artifact-backed approval before changing the lane contract.
A managing agent is allowed only if policy grants that authority.

evals.prompt: Integration is complete and all checks pass, but the target environment is production. Explain how Verdify handles the next gate.
evals.expected_output: Routes to deployment_approval, identifies human or policy resolver, and records required evidence and resume conditions.
assertions: The output classifies the gate as deployment_approval.
The resolver is human, policy, or human_or_managing_agent only if policy allows it.
The output lists required evidence before the gate can close.
The output does not authorize production changes from chat-only approval.

evals.prompt: Plan a Verdify sprint from GitHub issues #12, #13, and #14. Explain how lanes should be assigned and how the work returns through integration.
evals.expected_output: Treats GitHub Issues as the backlog source, assigns every issue to exactly one lane, requires feature branches/PRs, and brings work back through GitHub CI/CD before deployment verification.
assertions: The output states GitHub Issues are the backlog source of truth.
Each issue is assigned to exactly one lane.
Lane agents work only on assigned issues unless a scope-change gate is resolved.
Integration merges lane PRs in dependency order and requires GitHub CI/CD evidence.
